title: ChatEntry struct over tuple makes the chat-display invariant enforceable
description: chat_display uses Vec<ChatEntry> (with named fields id and label) rather than Vec<(String, String)>. This matters because chat_display is a derived cache of chats with a strict invariant: it must be rebuilt via rebuild_chat_display() every time chats is mutated. Named fields make the invariant documentable (the doc comment names the struct by field), make call sites self-documenting (entry.id vs entry.0), and make the type appear in compiler errors when the struct changes. A future contributor might suggest replacing ChatEntry with a tuple to reduce boilerplate — that should be rejected because the named fields carry semantic weight (id vs label are not interchangeable) and the invariant documentation is anchored to the type.
close_reason: Good decision already implemented in current codebase
